authoritative_sources.title: CMS Measures Inventory Tool
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS quality-measure inventory is authoritative for identifying CMS measure stewards, programs, numerator, denominator, exclusion, and specification references.

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Measures Management System Hub
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS quality-measure lifecycle guidance is authoritative for measure specification, implementation, maintenance, and change-control concepts that constrain quality-reporting claims.

authoritative_sources.title: eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal eCQI resource is authoritative for eCQM specifications, value sets, QRDA implementation guidance, and standards-based electronic quality-measure evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eCQM, QRDA, value-set, and validation handling
used_for: QRDA patient-level and aggregate file expectations

authoritative_sources.title: QualityNet
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS QualityNet is the official public portal for CMS quality reporting program resources, submission guidance, validation references, and program notices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Submission, validation, and program notice review

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 414.1340 - Data completeness criteria for the quality performance category
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal regulatory text defines quality data-completeness thresholds and explicitly treats selective, unrepresentative quality submissions as not true, accurate, or complete for MIPS purposes.

authoritative_sources.title: HEDIS Measures and Technical Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA stewards HEDIS and publishes recognized health-plan quality measure resources used for denominator, exclusion, data-source, and reporting workflow expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: HEDIS Compliance Audit Certification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA public audit guidance establishes HEDIS audit authority and confirms that audited submission workflows require certified audit ownership rather than self-certification by this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Specifications Manual for Joint Commission National Quality Measures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Joint Commission manual is a recognized public source for chart-abstracted measure definitions, data elements, abstraction logic, and accreditation quality-measure workflow.

authoritative_sources.title: Skilled Nursing Facility Quality Reporting Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS provider operating guidance shows quality reporting requirements, completeness expectations, payment-update consequences, and public Care Compare review posture for a facility program.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI uses, disclosures, and requests to the minimum necessary in quality reporting packages and escalations.

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ONC USCDI is the federal interoperability baseline for healthcare data classes used to trace patient, encounter, lab, problem, procedure, and clinical-operational quality evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Healthcare data lineage and terminology normalization

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Quality program, measure id, measure steward, specification version, program year, measurement period, reporting cadence, facility or plan context, payer/product, provider type, service setting, patient/member status, service dates, eligibility, referral, authorization, consent, abstraction ownership, submission deadline, source hierarchy, validation report, and signoff matrix gate safe reporting.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth set depends on the measure and tenant source hierarchy. EHR abstraction records, quality reporting platforms, eCQM engines, QRDA files, claims, encounters, labs, registries, HEDIS/IDSS extracts, BI marts, payer or regulator portal messages, validation reports, eligibility, referral, authorization, and privacy systems may each govern a different part of the reporting package.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at measure-version selection, source-lineage mapping, readiness caveats, reconciliation notes, and exception-register creation. Licensed clinical or abstraction owners decide undocumented clinical interpretation and source-record changes; privacy/compliance owners decide PHI disclosure; payer or utilization owners decide policy, coverage, and authorization interpretation; quality-program owners certify, attest, submit, or publish final results.

workflow_stages.name: Tenant, program, audience, and measure-context intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm quality program, measure id, measure steward, program year, measurement period, reporting cadence, payer/product, facility, provider type, service setting, intended audience, public-reporting posture, and signoff owners.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and authorization dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate patient/member status, eligibility, service dates, referral, authorization, consent, coverage/product context, documentation availability, abstraction owner, and retrieval hooks when they affect denominator or numerator logic.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Measure specification and version control
workflow_stages.description: Identify authoritative specification version, measure steward, data source method, numerator, denominator, exclusions, value sets, data-completeness thresholds, effective dates, and mid-cycle changes.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source collection, abstraction, and reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Collect and reconcile EHR abstraction, eCQM outputs, quality platform data, encounter, claim, lab, registry, BI, HEDIS, QRDA, and portal records while preserving conflicts and refresh windows.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Submission package assembly and validation review
workflow_stages.description: Assemble measure-specification summaries, numerator and denominator extracts, exclusion logic, validation findings, readiness notes, exception registers, and next-evidence requests.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Disclosure, attestation, and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop when PHI handling, low-volume disclosure, public-reporting language, clinical interpretation, payer interpretation, compliance review, quality attestation, formal submission, or leadership action is required.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: quality measure specifications, steward references, program-year guidance, measurement-period rules, value sets, numerator logic, denominator logic, exclusions, and data-completeness thresholds
EHR abstraction worksheets, chart abstraction files, clinical documentation references, encounter records, lab feeds, registry extracts, claims, eligibility, referral, and authorization records
eCQM engine outputs, CQL logic references, QRDA Category I patient-level files, QRDA Category III aggregate files, HEDIS extracts, IDSS submission artifacts, and quality platform exports
submission portal notices, QualityNet or payer/regulator validation reports, acceptance or rejection messages, error files, public-reporting preview records, and audit findings
measure-specification summaries, source-lineage maps, numerator and denominator extracts, exclusion reconciliation notes, submission-readiness notes, and quality-report exception registers
privacy, consent, minimum-necessary, low-volume disclosure, signoff, escalation, and audit-log records
systems_of_record: quality reporting platform or measure engine
EHR abstraction and clinical documentation system
enterprise data warehouse and governed BI semantic layer
claims, remittance, eligibility, referral, and authorization systems
laboratory information system and registry platforms
eCQM, QRDA, HEDIS, IDSS, payer, regulator, accreditation, and submission portal systems
privacy, consent, compliance, audit, approval, and public-reporting review systems

allowed: classify the quality reporting request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing healthcare prerequisites

choose the authoritative measure specification version, source hierarchy, denominator, numerator, exclusions, value-set references, data-completeness posture, and reporting cadence when sources support it
determine whether abstraction, mapping, validation, and source-lineage gaps prevent reliable submission-readiness claims
prepare measure-specification summaries, submission-readiness notes, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, and quality-report exception registers
escalate: required tenant facts, measure version, reporting program, patient/member status, payer or plan, facility type, service setting, eligibility, referral, authorization, privacy basis, retrieval hook, or signoff owner is missing
measure specifications, abstraction files, claims, EHR records, labs, registries, validation reports, payer rules, public guidance, or tenant policy materially conflict

PHI handling, low-volume disclosure, public-reporting posture, clinical risk, patient/member safety, payer interpretation, coding, compliance, legal, quality-program attestation, formal submission, or leadership approval is required
refuse: certify unsupported quality results, attest final submissions, or approve public quality statements
override official measure logic, value sets, inclusion rules, exclusions, data-completeness thresholds, or submission validation outcomes
imply diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, patient safety, clinical judgment, payer policy, coding, compliance, legal, or public-disclosure authority
publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, definitions, validation, or disclosure authority is unresolved
disclose PHI, identifiable examples, or low-volume cohorts without minimum-necessary and approval context

domain_failure_modes: treating quality reporting as generic dashboard reporting and failing to request measure id, specification version, program year, measurement period, steward, denominator, numerator, exclusions, value sets, or submission guide
using stale or mid-cycle measure logic without recording effective date, program-year applicability, and change-control impact
collapsing EHR abstraction, claims, labs, registries, QRDA, HEDIS, BI, and portal validation records into one truth set despite measure-specific source hierarchy
forcing numerator, denominator, or exclusion status when abstraction is incomplete or conflicts with claims, lab, or registry evidence
publishing quality, benchmark, outcome, public-facing, or regulatory claims without official measure logic, comparability evidence, validation status, and authorized signoff
disclosing PHI or low-volume quality gaps without minimum-necessary, consent, audience, and approval controls

letting the lane widen into licensed clinical judgment, payer-policy interpretation, coding approval, compliance/legal signoff, submission attestation, public disclosure approval, or operational leadership direction

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Clinical abstraction is incomplete or conflicts with claim data.
A stakeholder asks for a quality statement not supported by official measure logic.
Conflicting source records for a published metric.
Incomplete data that blocks reliable analysis.
Request for unsupported causal or policy claims.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific measure programs and payer or facility reporting rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Reporting requirements differ by payer, plan, product, facility type, accreditation program, registry, contract, and measurement year. The package treats those as runtime prerequisites and blocks or escalates when they are not supplied.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdictional privacy, public reporting, and disclosure requirements
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: State law, payer contracts, accreditation rules, and tenant policy can be stricter than the public baseline. The package requires tenant privacy and disclosure review before PHI, low-volume detail, public statements, or final submissions.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated specification-based package; variable tenant, payer, program, and jurisdictional details are explicit runtime prerequisites.

The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, coding approval, privacy-law interpretation, quality attestation, formal submission, public disclosure approval, and patient-safety judgment.
